1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم الم
1.english: Alif, Lam, Meem.
1.transliteration: Alif-Laaam-Meeem

1.arabic: غلبت الروم
1.english: The Byzantines have been defeated
1.transliteration: Ghulibatir Room

1.arabic: في أدنى الأرض وهم من بعد غلبهم سيغلبون
1.english: In the nearest land. But they, after their defeat, will overcome.
1.transliteration: Feee adnal ardi wa hummim ba'di ghalabihim sa ya ghliboon

1.arabic: في بضع سنين ۗ لله الأمر من قبل ومن بعد ۚ ويومئذ يفرح المؤمنون
1.english: Within three to nine years. To Allah belongs the command before and after. And that day the believers will rejoice
1.transliteration: Fee bid'i sineen; lillaahil amru min qablu wa mim ba'd; wa yawma'iziny yafrahul mu'minoon

1.arabic: بنصر الله ۚ ينصر من يشاء ۖ وهو العزيز الرحيم
1.english: In the victory of Allah. He gives victory to whom He wills, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Binasril laa; yansuru mai yashaaa'u wa Huwal 'Azeezur Raheem

1.arabic: وعد الله ۖ لا يخلف الله وعده ولكن أكثر الناس لا يعلمون
1.english: [It is] the promise of Allah. Allah does not fail in His promise, but most of the people do not know.
1.transliteration: Wa'dal laahi laa yukhliful laahu wa'dahoo wa laakin na aksaran naasi laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: يعلمون ظاهرا من الحياة الدنيا وهم عن الآخرة هم غافلون
1.english: They know what is apparent of the worldly life, but they, of the Hereafter, are unaware.
1.transliteration: Ya'lamoona zaahiram minal hayaatid dunya wa hum 'anil Aakhirati hum ghaafiloon

1.arabic: أولم يتفكروا في أنفسهم ۗ ما خلق الله السماوات والأرض وما بينهما إلا بالحق وأجل مسمى ۗ وإن كثيرا من الناس بلقاء ربهم لكافرون
1.english: Do they not contemplate within themselves? Allah has not created the heavens and the earth and what is between them except in truth and for a specified term. And indeed, many of the people, in [the matter of] the meeting with their Lord, are disbelievers.
1.transliteration: Awalam yatafakkaroo feee anfusihim; maa khalaqal laahus samaawaati wal arda wa maa bainahumaaa illaa bil haqqi wa ajalim musammaa; wa inna kaseeram minan naasi biliqaaa'i Rabbihim lakaafiroon

1.arabic: أولم يسيروا في الأرض فينظروا كيف كان عاقبة الذين من قبلهم ۚ كانوا أشد منهم قوة وأثاروا الأرض وعمروها أكثر مما عمروها وجاءتهم رسلهم بالبينات ۖ فما كان الله ليظلمهم ولكن كانوا أنفسهم يظلمون
1.english: Have they not traveled through the earth and observed how was the end of those before them? They were greater than them in power, and they plowed the earth and built it up more than they have built it up, and their messengers came to them with clear evidences. And Allah would not ever have wronged them, but they were wronging themselves.
1.transliteration: Awalm yaseeroo fil ardi fa-yanzuroo kaifa kaana 'aaqibatul lazeena min qablihim; kaanooo ashadda minhhum quwwatanw wa asaarul arda wa 'amaroohaaa aksara mimmaa 'amaroohaa wa jaaa'athum Rusuluhum bil baiyinaati famaa kaanal laahu liyazli mahum wa laakin kaanooo anfusahum yazlimoon

1.arabic: ثم كان عاقبة الذين أساءوا السوأى أن كذبوا بآيات الله وكانوا بها يستهزئون
1.english: Then the end of those who did evil was the worst [consequence] because they denied the signs of Allah and used to ridicule them.
1.transliteration: Summa kaana'aaqibatal lazeena asaaa'us sooo aaa an kazzaboo bi aayaatil laahi wa kaanoo bihaa yastahzi'oon

1.arabic: الله يبدأ الخلق ثم يعيده ثم إليه ترجعون
1.english: Allah begins creation; then He will repeat it; then to Him you will be returned.
1.transliteration: Allaahu yabda'ul khalqa summa yu'eeduhoo summa ilaihi turja'oon

1.arabic: ويوم تقوم الساعة يبلس المجرمون
1.english: And the Day the Hour appears the criminals will be in despair.
1.transliteration: Wa yawma taqoomus Saa'atu yublisul mujrimoon

1.arabic: ولم يكن لهم من شركائهم شفعاء وكانوا بشركائهم كافرين
1.english: And there will not be for them among their [alleged] partners any intercessors, and they will [then] be disbelievers in their partners.
1.transliteration: Wa lam yakul lahum min shurakaaa'ihim shufa'aaa'u wa kaanoo bishurakaaa'ihim kaafireen

1.arabic: ويوم تقوم الساعة يومئذ يتفرقون
1.english: And the Day the Hour appears - that Day they will become separated.
1.transliteration: Wa Yawma taqoomus Saa'atu Yawma'iziny yatafarraqoon

1.arabic: فأما الذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات فهم في روضة يحبرون
1.english: And as for those who had believed and done righteous deeds, they will be in a garden [of Paradise], delighted.
1.transliteration: Fa ammal lazeena aamanoo wa 'amilus saalihaati fahum fee rawdatiny yuhbaroon

1.arabic: وأما الذين كفروا وكذبوا بآياتنا ولقاء الآخرة فأولئك في العذاب محضرون
1.english: But as for those who disbelieved and denied Our verses and the meeting of the Hereafter, those will be brought into the punishment [to remain].
1.transliteration: Wa ammal lazeena kafaroo wa kazzaboo bi-Aayaatinaa wa liqaaa'il Aakhirati faulaaa'ika fil'azaabi muhdaroon

1.arabic: فسبحان الله حين تمسون وحين تصبحون
1.english: So exalted is Allah when you reach the evening and when you reach the morning.
1.transliteration: Fa Subhaanal laahi heena tumsoona wa heena tusbihoon

1.arabic: وله الحمد في السماوات والأرض وعشيا وحين تظهرون
1.english: And to Him is [due all] praise throughout the heavens and the earth. And [exalted is He] at night and when you are at noon.
1.transliteration: Wa lahul hamdu fis samaawaati wal ardi wa 'ashiyyanw wa heena tuzhiroon

1.arabic: يخرج الحي من الميت ويخرج الميت من الحي ويحيي الأرض بعد موتها ۚ وكذلك تخرجون
1.english: He brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living and brings to life the earth after its lifelessness. And thus will you be brought out.
1.transliteration: Yukhrijul haiya minal maiyiti wa yukhrijul maiyita minal haiyi wa yuhyil arda ba'da mawtihaa; wa kazaalika tukhrajoon

1.arabic: ومن آياته أن خلقكم من تراب ثم إذا أنتم بشر تنتشرون
1.english: And of His signs is that He created you from dust; then, suddenly you were human beings dispersing [throughout the earth].
1.transliteration: Wa min Aayaatiheee an khalaqakum min turaabin summa izaaa antum basharun tantashiroon

1.arabic: ومن آياته أن خلق لكم من أنفسكم أزواجا لتسكنوا إليها وجعل بينكم مودة ورحمة ۚ إن في ذلك لآيات لقوم يتفكرون
1.english: And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.
1.transliteration: Wa min Aayaatiheee an khalaqa lakum min anfusikum azwaajal litaskunooo ilaihaa wa ja'ala bainakum mawad datanw wa rahmah; inna fee zaalika la Aayaatil liqawminy yatafakkaroon

1.arabic: ومن آياته خلق السماوات والأرض واختلاف ألسنتكم وألوانكم ۚ إن في ذلك لآيات للعالمين
1.english: And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the diversity of your languages and your colors. Indeed in that are signs for those of knowledge.
1.transliteration: Wa min Aayaatihee khalqus samaawaati wal aardi wakhtilaafu alsinatikum wa alwaanikum; inna fee zaalika la Aayaatil lil'aalimeen

1.arabic: ومن آياته منامكم بالليل والنهار وابتغاؤكم من فضله ۚ إن في ذلك لآيات لقوم يسمعون
1.english: And of His signs is your sleep by night and day and your seeking of His bounty. Indeed in that are signs for a people who listen.
1.transliteration: Wa min Aayaatihee manaamukum bil laili wannahaari wabtighaaa'ukum min fadlih; inna fee zaalika la Aayaatil liqawminy yasma'oon

1.arabic: ومن آياته يريكم البرق خوفا وطمعا وينزل من السماء ماء فيحيي به الأرض بعد موتها ۚ إن في ذلك لآيات لقوم يعقلون
1.english: And of His signs is [that] He shows you the lightning [causing] fear and aspiration, and He sends down rain from the sky by which He brings to life the earth after its lifelessness. Indeed in that are signs for a people who use reason.
1.transliteration: Wa min Aayaatihee yureekumul barqa khawfanw wa tama'anw wa yunazzilu minas samaaa'i maaa'an fa yuhyee bihil arda ba'da mawtihaaa inna fee zaalika la Aayaatil liqawminy ya'qiloon

1.arabic: ومن آياته أن تقوم السماء والأرض بأمره ۚ ثم إذا دعاكم دعوة من الأرض إذا أنتم تخرجون
1.english: And of His signs is that the heaven and earth remain by His command. Then when He calls you with a [single] call from the earth, immediately you will come forth.
1.transliteration: Wa min Aayaatihee an taqoomas samaaa'u wal ardu bi-amrih; summa izaa da'aakum da'watam minal ardi izaaa antum takhrujoon

1.arabic: وله من في السماوات والأرض ۖ كل له قانتون
1.english: And to Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and earth. All are to Him devoutly obedient.
1.transliteration: Wa lahoo man fissamaawaati wal ardi kullul lahoo qaanitoon

1.arabic: وهو الذي يبدأ الخلق ثم يعيده وهو أهون عليه ۚ وله المثل الأعلى في السماوات والأرض ۚ وهو العزيز الحكيم
1.english: And it is He who begins creation; then He repeats it, and that is [even] easier for Him. To Him belongs the highest attribute in the heavens and earth. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazee yabda'ul khalqa summa yu'eeduhoo wa huwa ahwanu 'alaih; wa lahul masalul la'laa fissamaawaati wal-ard; wa Huwal 'Azeezul Hakeem

1.arabic: ضرب لكم مثلا من أنفسكم ۖ هل لكم من ما ملكت أيمانكم من شركاء في ما رزقناكم فأنتم فيه سواء تخافونهم كخيفتكم أنفسكم ۚ كذلك نفصل الآيات لقوم يعقلون
1.english: He presents to you an example from yourselves. Do you have among those whom your right hands possess any partners in what We have provided for you so that you are equal therein [and] would fear them as your fear of one another [within a partnership]? Thus do We detail the verses for a people who use reason.
1.transliteration: Daraba lakum masalam min anfusikum hal lakum mimmaa malakat aymaanukum min shurakaaa'a fee maa razaqnaakum fa antum feehi sawaaa'un takhaafoonahum kakheefa tikum anfusakum; kazaalika nufassilul Aayaati liqawminy ya'qiloon

1.arabic: بل اتبع الذين ظلموا أهواءهم بغير علم ۖ فمن يهدي من أضل الله ۖ وما لهم من ناصرين
1.english: But those who wrong follow their [own] desires without knowledge. Then who can guide one whom Allah has sent astray? And for them there are no helpers.
1.transliteration: Balit taba'al lazeena zalamooo ahwaaa'ahum bighairi 'ilmin famai yahdee man adallal laahu wa maa lahum min naasireen

1.arabic: فأقم وجهك للدين حنيفا ۚ فطرت الله التي فطر الناس عليها ۚ لا تبديل لخلق الله ۚ ذلك الدين القيم ولكن أكثر الناس لا يعلمون
1.english: So direct your face toward the religion, inclining to truth. [Adhere to] the fitrah of Allah upon which He has created [all] people. No change should there be in the creation of Allah. That is the correct religion, but most of the people do not know.
1.transliteration: Fa aqim wajhaka liddeeni Haneefaa; fitratal laahil latee fataran naasa 'alaihaa; laa taabdeela likhalqil laah; zaalikad deenul qaiyimu wa laakinna aksaran naasi laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: ۞ منيبين إليه واتقوه وأقيموا الصلاة ولا تكونوا من المشركين
1.english: [Adhere to it], turning in repentance to Him, and fear Him and establish prayer and do not be of those who associate others with Allah
1.transliteration: Muneebeena ilaihi wattaqoohu wa aqeemus Salaata wa laa takoonoo minal mushrikeen

1.arabic: من الذين فرقوا دينهم وكانوا شيعا ۖ كل حزب بما لديهم فرحون
1.english: [Or] of those who have divided their religion and become sects, every faction rejoicing in what it has.
1.transliteration: Minal lazeena farraqoo deenahum wa kaanoo shiya'an kullu hizbim bimaa ladaihim farihoon

1.arabic: وإذا مس الناس ضر دعوا ربهم منيبين إليه ثم إذا أذاقهم منه رحمة إذا فريق منهم بربهم يشركون
1.english: And when adversity touches the people, they call upon their Lord, turning in repentance to Him. Then when He lets them taste mercy from Him, at once a party of them associate others with their Lord,
1.transliteration: Wa izaa massan naasa durrun da'aw Rabbahum muneebeena ilaihi summa izaaa azaqahum minhu rahmatan izaa fareequm minhum be Rabbihim yushrikoon

1.arabic: ليكفروا بما آتيناهم ۚ فتمتعوا فسوف تعلمون
1.english: So that they will deny what We have granted them. Then enjoy yourselves, for you are going to know.
1.transliteration: Li yakfuroo bimaaa aatainaahum; fatamatta'oo fasawfa ta'lamoon

1.arabic: أم أنزلنا عليهم سلطانا فهو يتكلم بما كانوا به يشركون
1.english: Or have We sent down to them an authority, and it speaks of what they were associating with Him?
1.transliteration: Am anzalnaa 'alaihim sultaanan fahuwa yatakallamu bimaa kaanoo bihee yushrikoon

1.arabic: وإذا أذقنا الناس رحمة فرحوا بها ۖ وإن تصبهم سيئة بما قدمت أيديهم إذا هم يقنطون
1.english: And when We let the people taste mercy, they rejoice therein, but if evil afflicts them for what their hands have put forth, immediately they despair.
1.transliteration: Wa izaaa azaqnan naasa rahmatan farihoo bihaa wa in tusibhum sayyi'atum bimaa qaddamat aydeehim izaa hum yaqnatoon

1.arabic: أولم يروا أن الله يبسط الرزق لمن يشاء ويقدر ۚ إن في ذلك لآيات لقوم يؤمنون
1.english: Do they not see that Allah extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]? Indeed, in that are signs for a people who believe.
1.transliteration: Awalam yaraw annal laaha yabsutur rizqa limai yashaaa'u wa yaqdir; inna fee zaalika la Aayaatil liqawminy yu'minoon

1.arabic: فآت ذا القربى حقه والمسكين وابن السبيل ۚ ذلك خير للذين يريدون وجه الله ۖ وأولئك هم المفلحون
1.english: So give the relative his right, as well as the needy and the traveler. That is best for those who desire the countenance of Allah, and it is they who will be the successful.
1.transliteration: Fa aati zal qurbaa haqqahoo walmiskeena wabnassabeel; zaalika khairul lil lazeena yureedoona Wajhal laahi wa ulaaa'ika humul muflihoon

1.arabic: وما آتيتم من ربا ليربو في أموال الناس فلا يربو عند الله ۖ وما آتيتم من زكاة تريدون وجه الله فأولئك هم المضعفون
1.english: And whatever you give for interest to increase within the wealth of people will not increase with Allah. But what you give in zakah, desiring the countenance of Allah - those are the multipliers.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa aataitum mir ribal li yarbuwa feee amwaalin naasi falaa yarboo 'indal laahi wa maaa aataitum min zaakaatin tureedoona wajhal laahi fa ulaaa'ika humul mud'ifoon

1.arabic: الله الذي خلقكم ثم رزقكم ثم يميتكم ثم يحييكم ۖ هل من شركائكم من يفعل من ذلكم من شيء ۚ سبحانه وتعالى عما يشركون
1.english: Allah is the one who created you, then provided for you, then will cause you to die, and then will give you life. Are there any of your "partners" who does anything of that? Exalted is He and high above what they associate with Him.
1.transliteration: Allaahul lazee khalaqa kum summa razaqakm summa yumeetukum summa yuhyeekum hal min shurakaaa'ikum mai yaf'alu min zaalikum min shai'; Sub haanahoo wa Ta'aalaa 'ammaa yushrikoon

1.arabic: ظهر الفساد في البر والبحر بما كسبت أيدي الناس ليذيقهم بعض الذي عملوا لعلهم يرجعون
1.english: Corruption has appeared throughout the land and sea by [reason of] what the hands of people have earned so He may let them taste part of [the consequence of] what they have done that perhaps they will return [to righteousness].
1.transliteration: Zaharal fasaadu fil barri wal bahri bimaa kasabat aydinnaasi li yuzeeqahum ba'dal lazee 'amiloo la'allahum yarji'oon

1.arabic: قل سيروا في الأرض فانظروا كيف كان عاقبة الذين من قبل ۚ كان أكثرهم مشركين
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "Travel through the land and observe how was the end of those before. Most of them were associators [of others with Allah].
1.transliteration: Qul seeroo fil ardi fanzuroo kaifa kaana 'aaqibatul lazeena min qabl; kaana aksaruhum mushrikeen

1.arabic: فأقم وجهك للدين القيم من قبل أن يأتي يوم لا مرد له من الله ۖ يومئذ يصدعون
1.english: So direct your face toward the correct religion before a Day comes from Allah of which there is no repelling. That Day, they will be divided.
1.transliteration: Fa aqim wajhaka lid deenil qaiyimi min qabli any yaatiya Yawmul laa maradda lahoo minal laahi Yawma'iziny yassadda'oon

1.arabic: من كفر فعليه كفره ۖ ومن عمل صالحا فلأنفسهم يمهدون
1.english: Whoever disbelieves - upon him is [the consequence of] his disbelief. And whoever does righteousness - they are for themselves preparing,
1.transliteration: Man kafara fa'alaihi kufruhoo wa man 'amila saalihan fali anfusihim yamhadoon

1.arabic: ليجزي الذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات من فضله ۚ إنه لا يحب الكافرين
1.english: That He may reward those who have believed and done righteous deeds out of His bounty. Indeed, He does not like the disbelievers.
1.transliteration: Li yajziyal lazeena aamanoo wa 'amilus saalihaati min fadlih; innahoo laa yuhibbul kaafireen

1.arabic: ومن آياته أن يرسل الرياح مبشرات وليذيقكم من رحمته ولتجري الفلك بأمره ولتبتغوا من فضله ولعلكم تشكرون
1.english: And of His signs is that He sends the winds as bringers of good tidings and to let you taste His mercy and so the ships may sail at His command and so you may seek of His bounty, and perhaps you will be grateful.
1.transliteration: Wa min Aayaatiheee anyyursilar riyaaha mubashshi raatinw wa li yuzeeqakum mir rahmatihee wa litajriyal fulku bi amrihee wa litabtaghoo min fadlihee wa la'allakum tashkuroon

1.arabic: ولقد أرسلنا من قبلك رسلا إلى قومهم فجاءوهم بالبينات فانتقمنا من الذين أجرموا ۖ وكان حقا علينا نصر المؤمنين
1.english: And We have already sent messengers before you to their peoples, and they came to them with clear evidences; then We took retribution from those who committed crimes, and incumbent upon Us was support of the believers.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad arsalnaa min qablika Rusulan ilaa qawmihim fajaaa'oohum bil baiyinaati fantaqamnaa minal lazeena ajramoo wa kaana haqqan 'alainaa nasrul mu'mineen

1.arabic: الله الذي يرسل الرياح فتثير سحابا فيبسطه في السماء كيف يشاء ويجعله كسفا فترى الودق يخرج من خلاله ۖ فإذا أصاب به من يشاء من عباده إذا هم يستبشرون
1.english: It is Allah who sends the winds, and they stir the clouds and spread them in the sky however He wills, and He makes them fragments so you see the rain emerge from within them. And when He causes it to fall upon whom He wills of His servants, immediately they rejoice
1.transliteration: Allaahul lazee yursilur riyaaha fatuseeru sahaaban fa yabsutuhoo fis samaaa'i kaifa yashaaa'u wa yaj'aluhoo kisafan fataral wadqa yakhruju min khilaalihee fa izaaa asaaba bihee mai yashaaa'u min 'ibaadiheee izaa hum yastabshiroon

1.arabic: وإن كانوا من قبل أن ينزل عليهم من قبله لمبلسين
1.english: Although they were, before it was sent down upon them - before that, in despair.
1.transliteration: Wa in kaanoo min qabli any yunazzala 'alaihim min qablihee lamubliseen

1.arabic: فانظر إلى آثار رحمت الله كيف يحيي الأرض بعد موتها ۚ إن ذلك لمحيي الموتى ۖ وهو على كل شيء قدير
1.english: So observe the effects of the mercy of Allah - how He gives life to the earth after its lifelessness. Indeed, that [same one] will give life to the dead, and He is over all things competent.
1.transliteration: Fanzur ilaaa aasaari rahmatil laahi kaifa yuhyil arda ba'da mawtihaa; inna zaalika lamuhyil mawtaa wa Huwa 'alaa kulli shai'in Qadeer

1.arabic: ولئن أرسلنا ريحا فرأوه مصفرا لظلوا من بعده يكفرون
1.english: But if We should send a [bad] wind and they saw [their crops] turned yellow, they would remain thereafter disbelievers.
1.transliteration: Wa la'in arsalnaa reehan fara awhu musfarral lazalloo mim ba'dihee yakfuroon

1.arabic: فإنك لا تسمع الموتى ولا تسمع الصم الدعاء إذا ولوا مدبرين
1.english: So indeed, you will not make the dead hear, nor will you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs, retreating.
1.transliteration: Fa innaka laa tusmi'ul mawtaa wa laa tusmi'us summad du'aaa'a izaa wallaw mudbireen

1.arabic: وما أنت بهاد العمي عن ضلالتهم ۖ إن تسمع إلا من يؤمن بآياتنا فهم مسلمون
1.english: And you cannot guide the blind away from their error. You will only make hear those who believe in Our verses so they are Muslims [in submission to Allah].
1.transliteration: Wa maa anta bihaadil 'umyi 'an dalaalatihim in tusmi'u illaa mai yuminu bi aayaatinaa fahum muslimoon

1.arabic: ۞ الله الذي خلقكم من ضعف ثم جعل من بعد ضعف قوة ثم جعل من بعد قوة ضعفا وشيبة ۚ يخلق ما يشاء ۖ وهو العليم القدير
1.english: Allah is the one who created you from weakness, then made after weakness strength, then made after strength weakness and white hair. He creates what He wills, and He is the Knowing, the Competent.
1.transliteration: Allahul lazee khalaqa kum min du'fin summa ja'ala mim ba'di du'fin quwwatan summa ja'ala mim ba'di quwwatin du'fanw wa shaibah; yakhluqu maa yashaaa'u wa Huwal 'Aleemul Qadeer

1.arabic: ويوم تقوم الساعة يقسم المجرمون ما لبثوا غير ساعة ۚ كذلك كانوا يؤفكون
1.english: And the Day the Hour appears the criminals will swear they had remained but an hour. Thus they were deluded.
1.transliteration: Wa Yawma taqoomus Saa'atu yuqsimul mujrimoona maa labisoo ghaira saa'ah; kazaalika kaanoo yu'fakoon

1.arabic: وقال الذين أوتوا العلم والإيمان لقد لبثتم في كتاب الله إلى يوم البعث ۖ فهذا يوم البعث ولكنكم كنتم لا تعلمون
1.english: But those who were given knowledge and faith will say, "You remained the extent of Allah 's decree until the Day of Resurrection, and this is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not used to know."
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazeena ootul 'ilma wal eemaana laqad labistum fee kitaabil laahi ilaa yawmil ba'si fahaazaa yawmul ba'si wa laakinnakum kuntum laa ta'lamoon

1.arabic: فيومئذ لا ينفع الذين ظلموا معذرتهم ولا هم يستعتبون
1.english: So that Day, their excuse will not benefit those who wronged, nor will they be asked to appease [Allah].
1.transliteration: Fa Yawma'izil laa yanfa'ul lazeena zalamoo ma'ziratu hum wa laa hum yusta'taboon

1.arabic: ولقد ضربنا للناس في هذا القرآن من كل مثل ۚ ولئن جئتهم بآية ليقولن الذين كفروا إن أنتم إلا مبطلون
1.english: And We have certainly presented to the people in this Qur'an from every [kind of] example. But, [O Muhammad], if you should bring them a sign, the disbelievers will surely say, "You [believers] are but falsifiers."
1.transliteration: Wa laqad darabnaa linnaasi fee haazal Quraani min kulli masal; wa la'in ji'tahum bi aayatil la yaqoolannal lazeena kafaroo in antum illaa mubtiloon

1.arabic: كذلك يطبع الله على قلوب الذين لا يعلمون
1.english: Thus does Allah seal the hearts of those who do not know.
1.transliteration: Kazaalika yatba'ul laahu 'alaa quloobil lazeena laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: فاصبر إن وعد الله حق ۖ ولا يستخفنك الذين لا يوقنون
1.english: So be patient. Indeed, the promise of Allah is truth. And let them not disquiet you who are not certain [in faith].
1.transliteration: Fasbir inna wa'dal laahi haqqunw wa laa yastakhif fannakal lazeena laa yooqinoon
